content.caption: NC DIT logo.

content.description: Graphic showing three stages of 911 evolution: Yesterday (Location-CENTRIC), Today (Technology-CENTRIC), and Tomorrow (Citizen-CENTRIC).
content.caption: Yesterday Location-CENTRIC Enablers: Local Public Safety Resources Vendor Proliferation Benefit: Local control Today Technology-CENTRIC Enablers: Vendor consolidation Solidification of standards Statewide structure Benefit: Variety of options, Consistentcy of delivery Tomorrow Citizen-CENTRIC Enablers: Ubiquitous IP, GIS routing, Call analytics, Enhanced regional cooperation, Enhanced citizen demographics Benefit: User empowerment, Staffing flexibility, Lower costs

content.text:
• On track to achieve migration goal of 40 PSAPS in production on the NG911 network by EoY 2019
• 90%+ Project participation rate among approved PSAPs and growing
• Project goal is 100% migration to ESINet by EoY 2021
Live status of project updated frequently on website that will include GIS i3 migration as the project matures
911 Callers in the State of NC
Combines and Normalizes Local Data to obtain 98%+ Accuracy
GIS Data Repository Local GIS Data Managed by GeoComm
PSAPS NG 911 Call Delivery: Voice SMS Text R/T Video Pictures
AT&T ECRF/LVF Data Repository a/k/a EGDMS Enterprise Geospatial Database Management System
Combines ANI/ALI, MSAG, & Geospatial to 98%+ Accuracy Level

content.description: Flowchart diagram illustrating the North Carolina NG911 process, starting from callers, passing through GIS data repositories and ECRF/LVF data systems, to PSAPs. Features callout boxes describing process details like data normalization, accuracy, and policy routing rules.
content.caption: State of NC ESINet (AT&T)

content.text: NC NG911: The GIS view, con't

content.description: A workflow diagram illustrating the NC GIS integration for NG911. It shows multiple local GIS entities connected to a central repository, which then refines and combines data sets. This combined data is sent as a single data set to an AT&T ECRF/LVF network via a defined demarcation point. At the bottom right, there is an NC DIT logo.
content.caption: State of NC GIS integration plan for NG911 i3 call routing

cells.text:
1st Qtr, 2018
Bid development and bid solicitation

2nd-3rd Qtr, 2018
Bid receipt (6 respondents), bid evaluations, vendor presentations

4th Qtr, 2018
Bid finalists, phase two technical review and realignment

1st Qtr, 2019
BAFO and bid award to GeoComm

content.text:
Project Initiation
- Project Kickoff for NG911 GIS data assimilation : April 16, 2019
- Pilot PSAP GIS coordination work began May 3rd with kickoff call
- Regional Kickoff sessions begin June 4th through June 25th

**GIS Managed Services system: GIS Data Hub**
* Initial system installation and configuration
* Ongoing quality control feedback loop – GIS data and ALI/MSAG synchronization
* Ongoing statewide aggregated dataset updates and changeset deliveries to AT&T
**Stitch point layer along boundaries between jurisdictions**

**Optional:**
* Cell sector and cell site layer for the State depending on need and availability of resources
* GeoComm Maintainer ArcGIS Desktop toolbar
* GeoComm Resolver ArcGIS Desktop add-in for QC error management
